reasoning: The Bank Term Funding Program (BTFP) was created during the banking crisis in March 2023. It stopped extending new loans on March 11, 2024. As the maximum term for loans was one year, all outstanding loans matured by March 11, 2025. This is confirmed by the FRED time series data, which shows that the balance has been at exactly 0.0 from April 2025 through the end of March 2026. Because the program has been terminated and the balance is firmly at zero, there will be no new loans to increase the balance between March 29, 2026, and April 5, 2026. Therefore, the probability of an increase is functionally zero.
